mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity and enhances security while providing a scalable platform for various AI use cases. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential overhead in managing the infrastructure.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is essential for managing the deployment of Kagenti on your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary for your environment.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the credentials for the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on the Cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

```

> Drains all pods in the Kagenti namespace.
> Upgrades the Kagenti installation.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm repository may not be added correctly or the version is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Helm repo is added with 'helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti' and check the version.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be issues with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kagenti' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Kagenti deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or size in your GKE cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new AI agent protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed before proceeding.
